chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. Nos. D-611/2006 a/w CP No.D-1064/2005 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on CMA 7805/21 2. For orders on CMA 1070/21 3. For orders on Reference No.01/2018 4. For hearing of CMA 9541/09 5. For hearing of CMA 9542/09 Dated: 15.03.2021 Raja Qasit Nawaz Khan for petitioners. Mr. M. Nishat Warsi, Deputy Attorney General. -.-.- Urgency granted. Learned counsel for petitioners has pleaded that the orders as passed by this Court have not been complied with. We have asked learned counsel who is appearing for petitioners as to what was the order whereby this petition was disposed of. Learned counsel for petitioners has taken us to order dated 29.05.2009 in terms whereof this petition was disposed of by directing respondents to pay all their (petitioners’) VSS dues, which may be under VSS scheme offered by Petroman and accepted by the petitioners within a period of two months. Learned counsel submits that though VSS dues have been paid but salary amount has not been paid. We have asked learned counsel whether any observation in this regard was made by this Court, learned counsel has taken us to an undertaking given by learned Deputy Attorney General in paragraph 2 of such order. We have perused it and are of the opinion that this does not form part of the order as the only observation made by this Court is in the last paragraph whereby VSS dues were ordered to be settled and paid. Since in terms of order the VSS dues have already been settled and perhaps paid, therefore, no grievance can be raised by the petitioner as after passing of the order, referred above, the Court has become functus officio and nothing could be probed except compliance of order.

We have perused it and are of the opinion that this does not form part of the order as the only observation made by this Court is in the last paragraph whereby VSS dues were ordered to be settled and paid. Since in terms of order the VSS dues have already been settled and perhaps paid, therefore, no grievance can be raised by the petitioner as after passing of the order, referred above, the Court has become functus officio and nothing could be probed except compliance of order. Nothing additionally could be taken into consideration except what has already been decided on 29.05.2009. Learned counsel for petitioners then referred to order dated 01.10.2012 which relates to outstanding dues which may be under VSS and/or salary amount. Once VSS dues are settled and paid, all dues become part and parcel of such scheme hence nothing could be probed in these petitions which have already been disposed vide order dated 29.05.2009 as under VSS scheme all such dues have already been settled and paid accordingly. With these observations all pending applications stand disposed of. Judge Judge
